sourceDesc.p: Information about the three witnesses involved

div.note: The chosen lemma is usually the reading of F (indicated as "French" even if, of course, it is not Modern French), in order to have a common ground with the new digital edition of the whole textual tradition of Marco Polo's work coordinated by Eugenio Burgio (Università Ca' Foscari Venezia). The proper names appearing only in the German branch (resulting, for instance, from mistakes of the translator and/or the scribes) and not present elsewhere in the ms. tradition are necessarily kept in German (also in this case, the language in question is not Modern German). Common nouns (that is, terms and referential expressions), are presented, if possible, both in the reading of F and in their English translation. Later on, the English correspondents of all the toponyms and anthroponyms will be provided as well.

person.note: This name appears also in chapter 28 of DI, in the variant "Longiuchaam", this secondary reading originating already in branch b of TB.
Cf. VA: "del re che se ne fuzì"
I have not found any mention of Saint Veronica in any other redaction.

place.note: In the German translation it is introduced as a "land" from which the name of the city Baldracha originated. It looks like an innovation of DI, but the TB manuscripts still need to be checked.
This is interpreted as a place in the German translation but it actually does not exist as a place.
Actually, Camandi was a place close to Jiroft, but it no longer exists so the coordinates refer to the still existing centre.
Misunderstanding of the German translator: this toponym is used in DI to refer to the land in which the city of Charchan is.

place.placeName: A kingdom in Sumatra which remains unidentified

location.note: I viaggi di Marco Polo Veneziano (Vincenzo Lazari)

place.note: The toponym does not exist. It is actually a misunderstanding of Crecinam

location.note: Not to be mapped: probably originating from a misinterpretaion of the past participle of the verb "longare", "prolong". For the translator a place between Rhodes and the island of Euboea

place.note: "Corresponds in principle to the subarctic regions of northern Russia and western Siberia", see Lemmario, "Regione delle Tenebre"
The word is used as a toponym in the whole textual tradition, but it was originally the name of the people leaving South of Hindū Kūsh eand North of Kabul
